artPrompt: Conjured from a casserole-distracted shrug, one and a half tiny green-and-white voter sprites bloom upward in a tall column, the half-formed one fading mid-figure into a wash that bleeds off at the torso, ink line trailing into nothing. Golden light spills behind a sketched folding table and a dropped clipboard, all gesture and suggestion. A wholesome civic flicker of incompletion, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Half a Signature
text: Create one and a half 1/1 green and white Voter creature tokens. The half Voter has power and toughness 1/1 on the front and 0/0 on the back; flip the token over whenever someone at the table sighs. It counts as half a vote, which the rival mayor's recount clerk will absolutely contest.
flavorText: Bartholomew started signing, got distracted by the casserole, and never finished. He is one of our most reliable volunteers.

extras.halfThings: half a token, half a vote
